Version
  • API Reference
  • API Reference

API Reference

Weather Data

load_tmy(filepath::String) → TMYData

Load a Greenhouses-format TMY file (tab-separated, with # header lines).

interp_tmy(tmy::TMYData, t::Float64) → (T_out, I_glob, u_wind, T_sky)

Linearly interpolate weather data at time t [s]. Returns outdoor temperature [°C], global irradiance [W/m²], wind speed [m/s], and sky temperature [°C].

Parameter Structures

GreenhouseParams

Main parameter structure holding geometry, material properties, soil model, wall model, heating controller, and weather data.

FieldTypeDescription
AFloat64Floor area [m²]
ϕFloat64Roof slope [rad]
h_airFloat64Air zone height [m]
C_covFloat64Cover thermal capacity [J/K]
ε_covFloat64Cover emissivity [-]
C_airFloat64Air thermal capacity [J/K]
C_flrFloat64Floor thermal capacity [J/K]
ε_flrFloat64Floor emissivity [-]
soilSoilParamsSoil conduction model
A_wallsFloat64Total wall area [m²] (0 = no walls)
C_wallFloat64Wall thermal capacity [J/K]
FF_wall_skyFloat64Wall view factor to sky [-]
KpFloat64PI proportional gain [W/K] (0 = no heating)
TiFloat64PI integral time [s]
T_spFloat64Heating setpoint [K]
Q_maxFloat64Maximum heating power [W]
tmyTMYDataWeather data

Convenience constructors

GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=14000, ϕ=25°, h_air=3.8, Kp=0, Ti=300, T_sp=289.15, Q_max=0)

Creates a standard Venlo-type greenhouse with default glass and concrete properties. Set Kp > 0 to enable heating.

SavonaParams(tmy; with_walls=true, Kp=200, Ti=600, T_sp=291.15, Q_max=5000)

Creates the Savona 5×5 m greenhouse (Boccalatte et al. 2021) with asymmetric roof. Set with_walls=false to disable wall losses.

SoilParams

FieldTypeDescription
N_cIntNumber of concrete layers
N_sIntNumber of soil layers
λ_cFloat64Concrete conductivity [W/(m·K)]
λ_sFloat64Soil conductivity [W/(m·K)]
ρcp_cFloat64Concrete volumetric heat capacity [J/(m³·K)]
ρcp_sFloat64Soil volumetric heat capacity [J/(m³·K)]
AFloat64Area [m²]
T_deepFloat64Deep boundary temperature [K]

ODE System

greenhouse_rhs!(du, u, p::GreenhouseParams, t)

In-place right-hand side function for the ODE system. Compatible with any SciML solver:

prob = ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0, tspan, p)
sol = solve(prob, Tsit5(); reltol=1e-6)

initial_state(p; T0=278.15, T_soil0=276.15) → Vector{Float64}

Generate a consistent initial state vector. T0 sets the temperature of cover, air, floor (and walls if present). T_soil0 sets all soil layers.

Query Functions

FunctionReturnsDescription
state_size(p)IntLength of the state vector
has_walls(p)BoolWhether wall model is active
has_heating(p)BoolWhether PI heating is active
n_soil(p)IntNumber of soil layer states

Heat Transfer Functions

FunctionSignatureDescription
h_conv_ext(u, ϕ)(Float64, Float64) → Float64External convection coeff. [W/(m²·K)]
h_conv_free(dT, ϕ; floor)(Float64, Float64) → Float64Internal free convection coeff.
q_rad_T4(Ta, Tb, εa, εb; FFa, FFb)→ Float64LW radiative flux [W/m²]
solar_absorbed(I_glob)(Float64) → (Float64, Float64)Solar absorbed by cover and floor [W/m²]

PV Panels

PVPanel(τ, α, η, coverage, facade)

Full-control constructor. Fields:

FieldTypeDescription
τFloat64Solar transmittance (0 = opaque, 1 = fully transparent)
αFloat64Solar absorptance (fraction → heat + electricity)
ηFloat64Electrical efficiency at STC (25°C)
coverageFloat64Fraction of facade covered (0–1)
facadeSymbolWhich surface: :roof, :south_wall, :north_wall, :all_walls

Preset constructors

ConstructorταηDefault coverageTechnology
NoPanel()1.0000Bare glass
OpaquePanel(; coverage, η)00.850.201.0Crystalline Si
SemiTransparentPanel(; coverage, τ, η)0.40auto0.101.0Thin-film / OPV
TransparentPanel(; coverage, τ, η)0.65auto0.061.0BIPV glass

pv_cell_temperature(T_out, I_glob, u_wind) → T_cell

Faiman (2008) cell temperature model. Returns cell temperature in °C.

pv_efficiency(η_stc, T_cell, τ_panel) → η

Temperature-corrected efficiency. Uses β = −0.40%/°C for crystalline Si (τ=0), −0.25%/°C for thin-film (τ>0).

solar_absorbed(I_glob, panel, T_out, u_wind) → (R_cov, R_flr, Q_pv_heat, P_elec, T_cell)

Compute solar radiation distribution accounting for PV panel. Returns cover absorption, floor absorption, PV waste heat, electrical power (all W/m²), and cell temperature (°C).

has_pv(p) → Bool

Whether the greenhouse has active PV panels.

CSV Export

export_csv(filepath, sol, p)

Export simulation results to a human-readable CSV file.

Columns always present: time_h, T_air_C, T_cover_C, T_floor_C, T_out_C, T_sky_C, I_glob_Wm2, u_wind_ms.

Additional columns when walls are present: T_wall_C.

Additional columns when heating is active: Q_heat_Wm2, Q_heat_W, E_heat_kWh (cumulative).

export_csv("simulation.csv", sol, p)
# → "Results exported to: simulation.csv"
# → "  169 time steps, with heating"

Visualization

All visualization functions require a Makie backend loaded before GreenhousesJL:

using GLMakie          # or CairoMakie
using GreenhousesJL

plot_results(sol, p; save_path=nothing) → Figure

Plot time-series of the simulation on 2–3 panels:

PanelContent
1Temperatures: T_air, T_cover, T_floor, T_wall (if present), T_ext, setpoint
2Weather: solar irradiance (left axis), wind speed (right axis)
3Heating (if active): power W/m² (left axis), cumulative energy kWh (right axis)
plot_results(sol, p)                          # interactive window
plot_results(sol, p; save_path="out.png")     # static export

view_greenhouse_3d(p; save_path=nothing) → Figure

3D view of the greenhouse geometry. Shows:

  • Glass roof panels (transparent blue) with ridge (red) and gutters (grey)
  • Wall and gable surfaces (semi-transparent blue)
  • Floor slab (brown)
  • Geometry info label (area, height, wall area, setpoint)

Automatically detects Savona 5×5 m geometry (asymmetric 30°/60° roof) or renders a generic rectangular greenhouse.

view_greenhouse_3d(p)                           # interactive rotation
view_greenhouse_3d(p; save_path="serre.png")    # static export

Re-exported from SciML

For convenience, GreenhousesJL re-exports the following so that using GreenhousesJL is sufficient to set up and solve a simulation:

SymbolFromDescription
ODEProblemSciMLBaseODE problem constructor
solveSciMLBaseSolver dispatch
Tsit5OrdinaryDiffEqTsit5Adaptive 5th-order Runge-Kutta
